proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +30.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
If Bear regime returns abruptly, the existing UP_TRI-heavy book (12 of 22 opens) could see this boost amplify entries on the same signal_type — monitor regime_watch on rotation
Sample-bias check on the 96 trades not visible in derived views; if Bear days clustered narrowly, Wilson bound may overstate generalization
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +30.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +30.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. The cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy, so promotion defers activation until regime rotates — concentration risk on UP_TRI/Choppy is not compounded immediately. Consistent with prior approvals of the same cohort on 2026-04-29; no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +24.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
Prior cohort_promotion_judge approvals on 2026-04-29 for this exact cohort establish consistent precedent; no trader override observed.
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio is 100% Choppy-concentrated with 12 open UP_TRI positions; even sector-narrowed TAKE_SMALL boosts could marginally compound exposure if Metal UP_TRI signals fire during current regime.
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (project_anchor §7 D-8); true edge may compress if exit policy changes.
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +24.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +24.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) and promotion is correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than broadens the existing UP_TRI overweight in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and prior identical candidates were approved with consistent reasoning and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime = Choppy (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime = Choppy; recommended action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime shifts and exposure drops naturally via Day-6 exits, or (b) cohort tier promotes to Tier M. Specifically halt additions to DOWN_TRI×Auto and UP_TRI×Pharma cells. Consider partial hedge on the 4-position Auto short cluster given n=3 cohort support.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If regime flips from Choppy to Trend (Bull or Bear), all 22 positions face simultaneous adverse re-pricing with no validated edge to anchor recovery — both UP_TRI longs and DOWN_TRI shorts were entered under Choppy assumptions whose cohort statistics are not yet trustworthy.

evidence: regime_concentration triggered: 22/22 (100%) positions in Choppy, exceeds 80% threshold
Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort tier=Candidate, n=3 (very thin sample)
DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto cell at 18.18% (4 positions) — near 20% cell threshold with thinnest cohort backing
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in regime=Choppy until either (a) regime shifts and exposure drops naturally via Day-6 exits, or (b) cohort tier promotes to Tier M. Specifically halt additions to DOWN_TRI×Auto and UP_TRI×Pharma cells. Consider partial hedge on the 4-position Auto short cluster given n=3 cohort support.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If regime flips from Choppy to Trend (Bull or Bear), all 22 positions face simultaneous adverse re-pricing with no validated edge to anchor recovery — both UP_TRI longs and DOWN_TRI shorts were entered under Choppy assumptions whose cohort statistics are not yet trustworthy.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (above 80% threshold) and the dominant Choppy cohorts for both UP_TRI and DOWN_TRI map only to Candidate tier — unvalidated edge. Additionally, the DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto cell at 18.18% is approaching the 20% cell threshold with only n=3 cohort support, making the Auto short cluster particularly fragile.
